hook.text: Most caves are carved by water dissolving limestone, one drip at a time, over millions of years.

buildup.text: Rainwater absorbs carbon dioxide from the soil, becoming a weak acid.

discovery.text: This acid slowly eats through limestone, widening cracks into tunnels and chambers.

twist.text: A single drop of water can dissolve a grain of rock. Multiply that by billions of years.

climax.text: The world's longest cave system, Mammoth Cave, stretches over 680 km—carved entirely by water.

punchline.text: Patience and water can hollow out a mountain.

quiz.question: How do most limestone caves form?
options: Acidic water dissolves the rock over millions of years
